name: free_pcid_empty_implies_no_page_closure
code:
proof fn phi_2_free_pcid_empty_implies_no_page_closure(mm: MemoryManager, pcid: Pcid)
    requires
        mm.pagetables_wf(),
        0 <= pcid < PCID_MAX,
        mm.get_free_pcids_as_set().contains(pcid),
    ensures
        mm.page_tables@[pcid as int].unwrap().l3_tables@.dom() == Set::<PageMapPtr>::empty(),
        mm.page_tables@[pcid as int].unwrap().l2_tables@.dom() == Set::<PageMapPtr>::empty(),
        mm.page_tables@[pcid as int].unwrap().l1_tables@.dom() == Set::<PageMapPtr>::empty(),
        mm.page_tables@[pcid as int].unwrap().mapping_4k() == Map::<VAddr, MapEntry>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Free PCIDs have is_empty() tables, which guarantees empty lower-level tables and mappings — but the L4 table itself (cr3) still exists in page_closure, so "empty" tables still consume a page frame

name: pagetable_page_closure_always_nonempty
reason: page_closure includes l4_table@.dom() which always contains cr3 — even "empty" free page tables have a non-empty page_closure, meaning free PCIDs still hold allocated page frames that cannot be reclaimed

reason: iommutables_wf forces kernel_l4_end == 0 for all IOMMU tables — combined with is_empty checking indices [kernel_l4_end, 512), IOMMU is_empty checks ALL 512 L4 entries while process page tables skip kernel entries, creating an asymmetry that could mask bugs

name: disjoint_pcid_closures_no_shared_l4
code:
proof fn phi_5_disjoint_pcid_closures_no_shared_l4(mm: MemoryManager, pcid_i: Pcid, pcid_j: Pcid)
    requires
        mm.pagetables_wf(),
        0 <= pcid_i < PCID_MAX,
        0 <= pcid_j < PCID_MAX,
        pcid_i != pcid_j,
    ensures
        mm.page_tables@[pcid_i as int].unwrap().cr3 != mm.page_tables@[pcid_j as int].unwrap().cr3,
{
}
reason: Disjoint page_closures between distinct PCIDs implies distinct cr3 values — the spec enforces isolation at the page-frame level but this means no two address spaces can ever share an L4 root, even if intentional sharing were desired
